ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists for herbal luohan longan drink specifically; A0CKP (Longan, prepared/preserved) is the closest defensible parent category.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 ml (250 g) of herbal luohan longan drink, typically served warm or at room temperature in a cup or bowl.

nutrient_source.title: Longan, raw (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, FDC ID 168153) and Monk Fruit / Luo Han Guo herbal infusion estimation
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for herbal luohan longan drink exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy longan raw data (FDC ID 168153) diluted to reflect a typical brewed drink preparation (approximately 10–15% longan solids by weight in water), with rock sugar contribution estimated at approximately 5 g sugar per 100 ml. Luo han guo contributes negligible calories. This is a medium-confidence estimate. Per-100g values reflect the prepared beverage as served.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of longan and analogous tropical fruit-based sweet beverages — estimated from longan fruit GI data and sucrose-sweetened beverage GI literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for herbal luohan longan drink. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on: (1) longan fruit GI is approximately 50–60 per University of Sydney GI Database analogous tropical fruit entries; (2) the drink contains dissolved sugars from rock sugar (sucrose GI ~65) and longan solids; (3) the herbal components (luo han guo) contribute negligible glycemic load. A blended GI of 55 is a defensible estimate, placing it in the low GI category. Glycemic load per 250 g serving = (55 × 6.8 × 2.5) / 100 = 9.35, rounded to 9.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~2 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Herbal luohan longan drink is very low in sodium (~5 mg per 250 ml serving) and poses minimal concern for hypertension; however, individuals should be mindful of total fluid and sugar intake as part of an overall dietary pattern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Free sugar content from rock sugar and longan (~6.5 g sugar per 100 g; ~16 g per 250 ml serving)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume herbal luohan longan drink in moderation due to its free sugar content (~16 g per standard serving), which can raise postprandial blood glucose; portion control and infrequent consumption are advised, and sugar-reduced versions using luo han guo as the sole sweetener are preferable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat and cholesterol; low sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Herbal luohan longan drink contains negligible saturated fat and cholesterol and is low in sodium, making it generally acceptable for individuals managing cardiovascular disease, though regular consumption of sugar-sweetened versions should be limited to avoid excess caloric and glycemic load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Herbal Luohan Longan Drink (罗汉桂圆饮, Luo Han Gui Yuan Yin) is a traditional Chinese-Singaporean herbal sweet beverage prepared by simmering dried or fresh longan (gui yuan, Dimocarpus longan) together with luo han guo (monk fruit, Siraitia grosvenorii) and rock sugar in water. The drink is commonly served warm or at room temperature and is a staple of Chinese herbal beverage culture in Singapore, Malaysia, and Southern China. It has a naturally sweet, slightly floral flavour with mild herbal undertones from the monk fruit, and a light amber to golden-brown colour. The longan contributes a delicate fruity sweetness and subtle caramel notes, while the luo han guo adds a cooling herbal quality prized in Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) for its perceived throat-soothing and heat-clearing properties. Optional additions include red dates (hong zao) and wolfberries (goji berries), which enhance sweetness and add micronutrients. Nutritionally, the drink is a low-calorie, low-fat beverage with moderate sugar content derived primarily from rock sugar and the natural sugars of longan. Per 100 g of prepared drink, it provides approximately 28 kcal, 6.8 g carbohydrate (predominantly sugars at ~6.5 g), 0.2 g protein, and 0.1 g fat. Sodium content is very low (~2 mg per 100 g), making it suitable for low-sodium dietary patterns. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low), and a standard 250 ml serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 9.4. Clinically, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should moderate intake due to free sugar content; those managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease will find the drink generally acceptable given its negligible sodium and fat profile. The drink is naturally vegan, gluten-free, and halal-compliant.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for herbal luohan longan drink was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy longan raw data (FDC ID 168153) adjusted for dilution in a brewed beverage preparation with rock sugar. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database tropical fruit and sucrose-sweetened beverage data. Medium confidence is appropriate given the estimation basis.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy longan raw data (FDC ID 168153) diluted to reflect typical brewed drink preparation (~10–15% longan solids in water) with rock sugar (~5 g sugar per 100 ml). Direct laboratory analysis of this specific product is not available in approved sources. 2. GI is an analogous estimate of 55 (low) based on University of Sydney GI Database longan and tropical fruit entries and sucrose-sweetened beverage literature; no direct GI measurement for this drink exists in approved sources. 3. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this estimated beverage preparation. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the source database. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported for this diluted beverage preparation. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is confirmed zero as this is a plant-based herbal beverage containing no animal-derived ingredients. vitamin_d_mcg is confirmed zero as this beverage contains no vitamin D sources. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero per estimation from longan raw data; the fat content is negligible and saturated fraction is below detection threshold — confirmed zero. trans_fat_g is confirmed zero as no hydrogenated fats are present. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero as this is a plant-based beverage — naturally zero. 5. The food_group has been corrected from the input value of 'Grains and staples' to 'Beverages' as this is a liquid herbal drink. The cuisine has been reclassified from 'Filipino' to 'Chinese-Singaporean' as herbal luohan longan drink is a traditional Chinese herbal beverage widely consumed in Singapore and Southern China; the field survey listing at Lucky Cafe (North Canal Road, Singapore) is consistent with a Chinese-Singaporean café context. 6. Aliases consistency verified: all strings in aliases_by_language appear verbatim in the flat aliases list.
